business.headline: Why is Maharashtra's Rs 100 Will registration move getting so much attention?Anyone who owns assets such as a flat, land, jewellery, fixed deposits, shares or mutual funds should consider making a Will. Even small savings can become the reason for family disagreements if there is no clear-written instruction from the owner.

Pronto bags fresh $20 million funding, valuation jumps to $200 millionPronto offers quick home services such as laundry, kitchen preparation and home cleaning, with services starting from Rs 125. Workers are available within 15 minutes through the platform.

Stock market ends flat as Sensex slips 114, Nifty stays above 24,300Benchmark equity indices ended little changed on Wednesday, with the Sensex slipping 114 points and the Nifty holding above 24,300. The muted session underlined investor caution over global cues, interest rate expectations and earnings momentum, while selective buying capped losses.

Can senior citizens above skip ITR filing in FY27? Here's who qualifiesFiling income tax returns every year can be stressful for many elderly taxpayers. But did you know some senior citizens above 75 may not need to file ITR in FY27 at all if certain conditions are met?

Bought a car above Rs 10 lakh? You may be missing this tax creditCar buyers paying over Rs 10 lakh are being reminded to check the 1% TCS collected at purchase. The credit, linked to PAN records, can reduce tax dues or increase refunds at ITR filing.

Ex-CJI DY Chandrachud to mediate Sunjay Kapur family feud between mother, widowThe Supreme Court has pushed for a settlement in the Sunjay Kapur family trust dispute, with former CJI DY Chandrachud stepping in as mediator after both sides agreed to explore resolution through mediation.

DA hike explained: Why bank employees just got 0.70% while govt staff received 2%?A small DA hike for bank staff and a bigger one for government employees, has raised eyebrows. But is the difference unfair, or is there more to the calculation behind the scenes? Let us know here.

Explained: Why Paytm share price surged over 6% todayPaytm shares rose over 6% after One 97 Communications reported a Q4 profit and its first annual profit. The results signalled improving payments growth and margins even as regulatory concerns remained in focus.

Sensex, Nifty edge higher as softer crude lifts Dalal Street moodThe BSE Sensex rose 27.75 points, or 0.04%, to 77,986.27 in early trade, while the Nifty 50 gained 19.30 points, or 0.08%, to trade at 24,350.25 around 9:30 am.

8th Pay Commission: Govt employees push for 5-year pay hikes. Is it practical?As part of its exclusive 8th Pay Commission coverage, IndiaToday.in spoke to union leaders demanding salary revisions every five years. We also asked experts whether such a move would help employees or strain government finances further.

8th Pay Commission opens next round of employee consultations. Check key datesIn its latest circular dated May 6, 2026, the Commission said it will meet unions and associations linked to the Defence and Railway sectors in Delhi on May 13 and 14, 2026.

Can the US CLARITY Act finally bring clear rules to the crypto industry?For years, crypto has grown fast but without clear rules. The US CLARITY Act aims to fix that gap. Will it finally bring order to the digital asset space?

Internal SAIL notice shows plan to cut contract workforce by 40%. Read detailsAn internal SAIL (Steel Authority of India Limited) communication reviewed by India Today sheds light on why the PSU is planning a 40% reduction in contract workforce by 2027 and why unions are opposing the move.

HDFC Bank governance review finds no major lapses after chairman exit: ReportAn internal review at HDFC Bank found no material governance lapses after Atanu Chakraborty's exit. The findings are expected to steady investor confidence and clear the way for the CEO term proposal.
Sensex closes 940 points higher, Nifty jumps 24,300; IndiGo up 7%The S&P BSE Sensex jumped 940.73 points to close at 77,958.52, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 298.15 points to end at 24,330.95.

Salary hike or bonus in hand: Should you invest for returns or repay your home loan?A salary hike or bonus often feels like a reward, but it also brings a tough choice. Should you invest it to grow your wealth or put it towards repaying your home loan faster? Let's find out here.

Mukesh Ambani-led Reliance plans India's answer to Starlink: ReportReliance Industries is exploring a multi-billion-dollar LEO satellite venture under Jio Platforms to build an India-based rival to Starlink. The move could sharpen the satellite internet race as India pushes for domestic space and broadband capacity.

Govt approves ECLGS 5.0 to support MSMEs, airlines amid global tensionsUnder Emergency Credit Line Guarantee Scheme 5.0, the government will offer 100% credit guarantee for MSMEs and 90% for non-MSMEs and airlines.

Confused about ITR filing, refunds or deductions? 'Kar Saathi' has answersFiling your ITR and stuck with questions on refunds or deductions? You're not alone—and now there's an easier way to get help. Could 'Kar Saathi' be the answer you've been looking for?

Memes, reels and trades: How social media hype is driving risky stock betsBillionaire investor Warren Buffett recently said in a podcast that he no longer fully understands parts of today's market and even likened it to a casino for investors. IndiaToday.in spoke to experts to understand whether markets are increasingly being driven by speculation rather than fundamentals.

entertainment.headline: We are not done yet: Dhurandhar producer Jyoti Deshpande teases surprise for fans Jyoti Deshpande, who co-produced Dhurandhar and Dhurandhar: The Revenge alongside Aditya Dhar, shared her plans for a surprise for fans later this year.

Aishwarya Rai, Alia Bhatt, Aditi Rao Hydari set to attend Cannes Film Festival 2026 Aishwarya Rai Bachchan, Alia Bhatt and Aditi Rao Hydari are set for Cannes Film Festival 2026. Their presence underscores India's blend of cinema, fashion and women's empowerment on a global stage.

Vidya Balan emotional watching 25-year-old film The Last Tenant co-starring Irrfan Vidya Balan watched The Last Tenant for the first time since shooting it 25 years ago with Irrfan. She shared her emotional reaction as she revisited a difficult phase in her early career.

Vijay Varma starrer Matka King to return with Season 2. Read details here Vijay Varma's Matka King has officially been renewed for a second season after the success of Season 1. The OTT platform has confirmed the announcement on social media with a new poster teasing the next chapter of the crime drama.

Sanjay Dutt's Aakhri Sawal: CBFC clears film, trailer on May 8. See release date Sanjay Dutt's Aakhri Sawal has secured CBFC clearance, and the film's theatrical release date has been finalised. The trailer is scheduled to be out on May 8.

Netflix, ZEE5 this week: Bharathanatyam 2, Love Mocktail 3 and more Netflix and ZEE5 are bringing a fresh lineup of films and series this week. From Bharathanatyam 2: Mohiniyattam to My Royal Nemesis, viewers can expect a mix of suspense, action and heartfelt storytelling.

Insult to Tamil Nadu: Kamal Haasan strongly defends Vijay's right to form government Kamal Haasan said Vijay, as leader of the single largest party, should be invited to form the government in Tamil Nadu. He said the majority must be tested on the Assembly floor, warning that any delay disrespects the public mandate.

Robert Downey Jr rejects influencers as stars of future, avoids the 'rabbit hole' Actor Robert Downey Jr. has dismissed the idea that social media influencers are the stars of the future. He said people can now become celebrities without doing much besides pointing a phone at themselves.

Prime Video, JioHotstar this week: Dacoit, Lukkhe, No Place to be Single and more Prime Video and JioHotstar have a fresh lineup of films and shows releasing this week. From action thrillers like Dacoit to emotional dramas like Song Sung Blue, the list includes entertainment across different languages and genres.

Vijay's family tree: Meet the people behind Tamil Nadu's CM-in-waiting As Vijay prepares to take office in Tamil Nadu, attention has turned to his family and film roots. Their story traces the personal and cinematic links that ran alongside his political rise.

Palaash Muchhal disables Instagram comments after Smriti's friend files case Palaash Muchhal visited the Khatu Shyam temple and later switched off comments on his Instagram post as reports emerged of a caste-related case being filed against him. The development came amid growing attention around the complaint and shortly after his wedding to Smriti Mandhana was reportedly called off.

Kartavya trailer: Saif Ali Khan caught between duty and family in Netflix film Netflix has unveiled the trailer of Kartavya, starring Saif Ali Khan as a police officer under pressure after a journalist is shot under his watch. The film sets up a clash between duty and family as the investigation draws him into a wider web of power and deception.

Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaram postponed to June to avoid IPL clash Samantha's Maa Inti Bangaram has been postponed from May 15 to June to avoid the IPL season. The shift comes as promotions gather pace around her return to Telugu cinema and the film's action-heavy pitch.

Let Vijay form the government: Prakash Raj slams Governor over delay Prakash Raj, in an exclusive chat with India Today, said Vijay must be invited to form the government and prove his majority on the floor of the House. He criticised the Governor's delay while saying Vijay must now answer doubts over governance and administration.

Vivek Agnihotri marks one year of Operation Sindoor, pays tribute to Indian army Vivek Agnihotri marked one year of Operation Sindoor with a tribute to India's leadership, armed forces and citizens, while reflecting on the courage behind the mission.

Bandar teaser: Bobby Deol plays fading star caught in scandal and chaos The teaser of Bobby Deol's Bandar offers a dark glimpse into the chaotic life of a fading television star accused of a serious crime. Directed by Anurag Kashyap, the crime thriller also stars Sanya Malhotra and Saba Azad.

Daredevil Born Again 2 finale review: Heartbreaking price of street vigilantism Do Matt Murdock and Karen Page bring down Wilson Fisk in court as Hell's Kitchen erupts? And what is Matt's personal cost of vigilantism? Season 2 finale has answers but also leaves space for the adventure to continue.

Viral: BTS meets Mexican President, waves to 50,000 fans from palace balcony BTS met Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum before greeting fans from the National Palace balcony. The appearance drew nearly 50,000 fans within five hours and quickly became a major online moment.

Priyanka Chopra Jonas, Orlando Bloom to star in Matt Smukler's thriller Reset Priyanka Chopra Jonas and Orlando Bloom are set to headline the upcoming survival thriller Reset directed by Matt Smukler. The film follows a woman stranded in the wilderness who must rely on a mysterious stranger to survive.

general news.headline: Vijay's TVK warns all 107 MLAs will resign if DMK, AIADMK form govt: SourcesAmid the ongoing political deadlock in Tamil Nadu, the Vijay-led Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK) is considering a mass resignation of all its 107 MLAs if either the DMK or the AIADMK is invited to form the government, party sources said.

Nida Khan arrested after 25-day hunt in TCS Nashik sexual harassment caseNida Khan is accused of hurting religious sentiments and allegedly helping those accused in rape and sexual exploitation cases linked to TCS Nashik. Investigators have also alleged that she attempted to influence one of the complainants to adopt Islam and discouraged survivors from filing complaints against the accused.

2 Indian crew aboard ship with deadly hantavirus outbreak, their status unknownThree people have died and eight others have been infected aboard the MV Hondius, where two Indian crew members remain on board as authorities race to contain a rare hantavirus outbreak at sea.

Bengal Assembly dissolved, Mamata Banerjee no longer Chief MinisterMamata Banerjee had been stubborn in holding onto her Chief Minister's chair. But she is no longer the head of Bengal as Governor RN Ravi used his constitutional powers on Thursday to dissolve the state assembly.

Move aside, Mr Governor. Vijay has a mandateThalapathy Vijay's TVK was voted by the people of Tamil Nadu as the single biggest party. The Governor must give him a chance to form the government. That is the convention and judicial interpretation of the Supreme Court, and Vijay shouldn't be kept waiting.

Kerala MLA resists as Congress leader tries to hug her, video sparks rowIn a now viral video, a Kerala Congress leader is seen trying to hug a newly-elected woman MLA, ignoring the latter's attempt to evade the embrace and push him away.

Rat poison in watermelon killed family of 4 in Mumbai, finds probeForensic tests found zinc phosphide in the Dokadia family's viscera and the watermelon they ate on the ill-fated night. The findings have shifted the Mumbai Police probe from suspected food poisoning to tracing how the toxin entered the food.

Iran clamps down on Hormuz with strict new transit rules: ReportIran has set up a new body, the Persian Gulf Strait Authority (PGSA), to manage traffic through the strait. It has issued a form called "Vessel Information Declaration", which ships must complete before transit.

Assembly dismissed, Mamata no more CM. Who's in charge in Bengal?Bengal has entered a constitutional grey zone as the term of the current Assembly automatically ended today. A fiery Mamata Banerjee has refused to resign. The development has placed Bengal Governor RN Ravi at the centre of attention.

Mahua Moitra faces TMC chor, Jai Shri Ram chants on IndiGo flight, claims harassmentTMC MP Mahua Moitra alleged that she was harassed by a group of men onboard an IndiGo flight to Delhi, who shouted slogans like "chor chor, TMC chor" and "Jai Shri Ram", while filming her inside the aircraft.

What happened on hantavirus-hit cruise ship? Vlogger recalls horror voyageTurkish vlogger Ruhi Cenet has described how a hantavirus outbreak unfolded aboard the MV Hondius during a South Atlantic expedition. His account has raised questions over delayed precautions, onboard medical readiness and possible exposure at Tristan da Cunha.

What is zinc phosphide, the rat poison that killed a family in Mumbai?Zinc phosphide rat poison, not watermelon, killed the Dokadia family of four in Mumbai's Pydhonie area. There is no antidote for it, and doctors have very little they can do.

Inside the Puducherry luxury resort where AIADMK MLAs are being kept togetherAs Tamil Nadu's fractured mandate triggers frantic backroom negotiations, the AIADMK has shifted its MLAs to a luxury beachside resort in Puducherry.

Vijay has 107 MLAs, Governor insists on 118: What does the Constitution say?Constitutional experts are divided on whether the Tamil Nadu governor must first invite Vijay's TVK to form the government. The dispute has revived wider questions about the governor's discretion, constitutional convention and the floor test.

Turbulent Friday: Half of India will be on fire, other half to get drenchedThe IMD has forecast thunderstorms, gusty winds and heatwave conditions across several parts of India on Friday. The warning underlines a sharp pre-monsoon split, with storms in the hills and south and rising heat in the west.

Putin tightens security as Kremlin fears assassination threats amid Ukraine warVladimir Putin has reportedly tightened security around his movements and residences after claims of a threat near his Novgorod home. The measures reflect rising concern in the Kremlin over senior officials' vulnerability as the Ukraine war intensifies.

Aishwarya Rai, Alia Bhatt, Aditi Rao Hydari set to attend Cannes Film Festival 2026Aishwarya Rai Bachchan, Alia Bhatt and Aditi Rao Hydari are set for Cannes Film Festival 2026. Their presence underscores India's blend of cinema, fashion and women's empowerment on a global stage.

INDIA bloc is gone: DMK after Congress dumps alliance, ties up with Vijay's TVK"INDIA bloc is gone," was the response from an upset DMK after the Congress decided to break all ties with the MK Stalin-led party and joined hands with actor-politician Vijay's TVK in its bid to form a government in Tamil Nadu.
Mamata Donald BanerjeeThere are similarities between Donald Trump of November 2020, and Mamata Banerjee of May 2026. Their self-delusion and choreography are identical.

US to resume Hormuz op as Saudi, Kuwait lift curbs on military bases, airspace: WSJThe United States is preparing to restart Project Freedom in the Strait of Hormuz after Saudi Arabia and Kuwait reportedly restored access to military bases and airspace.

The risks of not having Suvendu Adhikari as the West Bengal CMUnion Home Minister Amit Shah is set to fly to Kolkata today to oversee the selection of West Bengal's next Chief Minister after the BJP's landslide victory. While several names are doing the rounds, Suvendu Adhikari is the frontrunner. It will be a big risk to not appoint him the CM. Not for the BJP as a party, but for its messaging and impetus.

US-Iran tensions escalate as ceasefire comes under pressure in Strait of HormuzFresh fighting broke out between the United States and Iran as Tehran reviewed a US-backed proposal to end the conflict. The exchange renewed fears over maritime security, energy supplies and the durability of the ceasefire.

Dutch woman travelling solo in India gets stranded on highway, gets help from 3 menA Dutch motorcyclist travelling solo in India was helped by three local men after a puncture left her stranded on a highway.

Woman cop goes undercover at Hyderabad bus stand at night, 40 men approached herMalkajgiri Commissioner V Sumathi went undercover at a Dilsukhnagar bus stop late at night. The exercise tested night patrols and exposed risks faced by women commuting alone.

We are not done yet: Dhurandhar producer Jyoti Deshpande teases surprise for fansJyoti Deshpande, who co-produced Dhurandhar and Dhurandhar: The Revenge alongside Aditya Dhar, shared her plans for a surprise for fans later this year.

Aishwarya Rai, Alia Bhatt, Aditi Rao Hydari set to attend Cannes Film Festival 2026Aishwarya Rai Bachchan, Alia Bhatt and Aditi Rao Hydari are set for Cannes Film Festival 2026. Their presence underscores India's blend of cinema, fashion and women's empowerment on a global stage.

sports.headline: Is Varun Chakravarthy's IPL blip a case of T20 World Cup fatigue? KKR bowling coach Dwayne Bravo feels Varun Chakravarthy may still be feeling the effects of India's hectic T20 World Cup-winning campaign. Bravo said the mystery spinner probably needed a proper mental and physical break before IPL 2026.

How Prince Yadav used Virat Kohli's advice against him to stun RCB legend IPL 2026, LSG vs RCB: Prince Yadav revealed he used advice from Virat Kohli himself to dismiss the batting star for a two-ball duck in Lucknow Super Giants' 9-run (DLS) win over the Royal Challengers Bengaluru at the Ekana Stadium.

Mitchell Marsh and Prince Yadav finally wake Ekana up as LSG end home nightmare IPL 2026: Mitchell Marsh's hundred and Prince Yadav's timely spell carried Lucknow Super Giants to a nine-run DLS win over Royal Challengers Bengaluru at Ekana Stadium. The result ended LSG's long home slide and kept their playoff hopes alive.

Babar Azam suffers injury blow after PSL success, ruled out of 1st Test vs Bangladesh BAN vs PAK, 1st Test: Pakistan were dealt with a huge blow after their premier batter Babar Azam was ruled out of the opening Test, starting Friday, May 8, due to a knee injury.

Bangladesh board suspends BPL figures over betting and obstruction charges The Bangladesh Cricket Board has charged several players, team officials and franchise representatives over alleged betting-related activities and obstruction of investigations linked to the Bangladesh Premier League.

Too little, too late? Mitchell Marsh hits 2nd IPL century amid LSG's early exit threat IPL 2026, LSG vs RCB: Mitchell Marsh smashed the fastest century by a Lucknow Super Giants batter in the history of the tournament. But has it come too late for LSG given that they are on the cusp of an early elimination?

Can Vinesh compete in comeback event? WADA rule for retired athletes raises question Olympian Vinesh Phogat's proposed return to competition at the Gonda ranking tournament remains clouded by uncertainty over WADA's six-month notice rule for retired athletes. The lack of clarity has also cast serious doubt over whether the event can aid her qualification push for the 2026 Asian Games.

IPL likely to expand to 94 games from 2028, says league chairman Arun Dhumal The BCCI is planning a 94-match IPL from 2028 while retaining 10 teams. The shift depends on a longer ICC window and aims to simplify the format for fans and broadcasters, league chairman Arun Dhumal said.

Jannik Sinner hits out over French Open prize money debate: Don't feel any respect Italian Open 2026: Jannik Sinner criticised the French Open prize money structure, saying players feel disrespected despite contributing heavily to the sport's growth. The World No.1 also backed player concerns over representation, pensions and health support.

Real Madrid meltdown? Valverde taken to hospital after spat with Tchouameni Spanish reports said Federico Valverde was taken to hospital after a training-ground altercation with Aurelien Tchouameni at Real Madrid. The episode reportedly triggered an emergency dressing-room meeting as pressure mounted before El Clasico.

Video: 345 sq ft handmade artwork of Virat Kohli unveiled outside Lucknow stadium IPL 2026: Fans unveiled a giant handmade Virat Kohli artwork outside the Ekana Stadium before RCB's match against LSG on Thursday. The scene underlined Kohli's popularity in Lucknow.

Suryakumar Yadav and wife Devisha Shetty welcome baby girlIPL 2026: Suryakumar Yadav and Devisha Shetty have welcomed a baby girl during the IPL season. The news has drawn wishes from across cricket and left Mumbai Indians awaiting clarity on his next-game availability.

Bangladesh vs Pakistan Test series: Squads, schedule and all you need to knowBAN vs PAK, 1st Test: Najmul Hossain Shanto's Bangladesh and Shan Masood's Pakistan are all set to lock horns in a two-match Test series, starting Friday, May 8 at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium in Mirpur.

Gambhir handling dressing room with a stick, players not happy with him: Ex-cricketerFormer cricketer Atul Wassan has accused Gautam Gambhir of bullying behaviour and a rigid management style in the India dressing room. His remarks renew focus on dressing-room tensions despite India's recent white-ball titles.

Ashes rivals England set to appoint ex-Australian cricketer as men's selectorEngland are set to appoint former Australia international Marcus North as their new national selector, with the ex-Ashes rival poised to become the first overseas figure to oversee selection for the England men's team.

England captain Ben Stokes ready to bowl on cricket comeback from injuryBen Stokes is expected to contribute with both bat and ball for Durham as the England captain returns to competitive action for the first time since England's disastrous Ashes campaign.

10,000 extra tickets for Karnataka VIPs? BCCI shocked by demands for IPL finalThe BCCI said Bengaluru lost the hosting rights for the IPL 2026 final after the Karnataka State Cricket Association allegedly demanded nearly 10,000 additional complimentary tickets beyond the permitted 15 percent quota.

Ban on girlfriends travelling with players? BCCI set to impose strict IPL rulesBCCI has warned IPL franchises over unauthorised individuals travelling and staying with teams. The board says stricter rules and "very stringent action" will follow any future violations during IPL 2026.

LSG vs RCB Highlights: Lucknow stay alive in playoffs race after thrilling win vs RCBLSG (Lucknow) vs RCB (Bengaluru) Highlights, IPL 2026: Lucknow Super Giants stayed alive in the race to the playoffs after beating the Royal Challengers Bengaluru by 9 runs via DLS at the Ekana Stadium in Lucknow.

MS Dhoni is CSK. Be careful in wishing him to retire: Clarke warns Thala criticsIPL 2026: Michael Clarke has urged fans not to push MS Dhoni towards retirement as the CSK icon remains out of IPL 2026 with a calf strain. His remarks come as Chennai regain momentum without Dhoni, deepening uncertainty over whether he will return this season.

Was Yuzvendra Chahal caught vaping on a flight? Viral video sparks buzzA video circulating online allegedly shows Yuzvendra Chahal using an e-cigarette on a Punjab Kings flight. This becomes the second incident of vaping in the IPL circles.

Vaibhav to retire burnt out by 21? English greats make bold predictionFormer England cricketers Michael Vaughan, Phil Tufnell, Alastair Cook and David Lloyd were left stunned by Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's explosive IPL 2026 campaign, with the former England stars joking on the Stick to Cricket podcast that the 15-year-old could be "burnt out by 21" and rich enough to retire early given the astonishing pace of his rise.

Will the FIFA World Cup be broadcast in India? AIFF says market too big to missThe All India Football Federation expects FIFA and Indian broadcasters to strike a World Cup 2026 deal soon. With the tournament a month away, valuation gaps and late-night match timings remain the main hurdles.

How Klaasen dropped 300 strike-rate mentality for new SRH role in IPL 2026After SRH's win over PBKS, Heinrich Klaasen said he has stopped chasing a 300 strike-rate and is batting according to the situation. The shift has brought him the Orange Cap and underlined Hyderabad's climb to the top at the right time.

Iran wants FIFA guarantees before participating in World Cup in United StatesIran FA chief Mehdi Taj will meet Gianni Infantino to seek assurances that Iran will be treated with respect at the World Cup in the United States. The talks have become central to Iran's participation plans after Canada refused Taj entry and Tehran warned it could reconsider playing in the tournament.

Australia stars say no to Pakistan ODI tour, prefer to stay back in IPLAustralia are expected to tour Pakistan and Bangladesh without Pat Cummins, Josh Hazlewood and Mitchell Starc as the trio prioritise IPL play-off commitments with their franchises.

Pant needs clarity of thought: Pujara baffled by LSG captain's poor IPL recordCheteshwar Pujara said it was baffling that a naturally attacking batter like Rishabh Pant has not been able to replicate his Test success in the IPL, where his numbers remain underwhelming by his standards.

Kohli pays tribute to U-19 teammate Amanpreet Singh Gill after he dies at 36Virat Kohli led tributes for his former Under-19 teammate Amanpreet Singh Gill, who died aged 36. Messages from Yuvraj Singh and Punjab Kings underlined the respect Gill commanded across Punjab cricket.

Cummins offered 4 million dollar deal by Australia to rival IPL's big bucksPat Cummins has reportedly been offered a 4 million Australian dollar annual contract by Cricket Australia as the board looks to counter the growing financial pull of IPL and franchise cricket.

Why Connolly's SRH hundred meant more than just runs: PBKS spin coach explainsCooper Connolly's fighting century against Sunrisers Hyderabad drew strong praise from PBKS spin-bowling coach Sairaj Bahutule. The innings underlined Punjab's faith in their No. 3 and strengthened the view that he can go a long way for Australia.

SRH vs PBKS: How did Hyderabad plot to stop Punjab's dangerman Priyansh Arya?SRH vs PBKS: Pat Cummins dismissed Arya with a well-directed short ball as SRH assistant coach James Franklin revealed Hyderabad had specifically planned around the PBKS opener's preferred scoring areas.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
